creator: amdmi3@amdmi3.ru
text:
* Thomas E. Zander (riggs@rrr.de) wrote:

> From your log, I see two possible problems. First, right after the
> beginning, your mplayer says
> "This codecs.conf is too old and incompatible with this MPlayer
> release! at line 6"
> How does that happen? We don't install a codecs.conf file for ages. Do
> you have one in your ~/.mplayer folder?
I do. Can't remember for what reason, but removing it doesn't change
anything.

> The other possible problem is of course the message
> "Error: Shared object "libstdc++.so.5" not found, required by "drvc.so"
>  Error loading dll
>  ERROR: Could not open required DirectShow codec drvc.so."
> 
> Remember that drvc.so is not a native lib:
> ~ > file /usr/local/lib/win32/drvc.so
> /usr/local/lib/win32/drvc.so: ELF 32-bit LSB shared object, Intel 80386, version 1 (SYSV), stripped
>
> You do need to have the linux ABI module loaded and linux_base
> installed. It should provide the necessary libstdc++.

That's a surprise. As far as I remember, it sure worked (i.e. .rm files
played) without linux_base. Not sure for linux.ko.

Anyway the problem disappeared for me. After some digging I found
out that it works only when compat6x is installed, but after reboot
it works even without compat6x. Strange enough.

So currently it still spit out some errors:

--

but it does work without any problems (and without linux and compat6x).
So the PR could be closed, thanks.
